text: A 68-year-old man was diagnosed with muscle-invasive bladder cancer (predominantly urothelial histology) via TURBT performed 3 weeks ago. Chest CT and CTU showed no evidence of metastasis (N0M0). ECOG performance status is 0, assessed 2 days ago. Recent screening labs (7 days ago) show adequate renal and hepatic function (creatinine 0.9 mg/dL, ALT 22 U/L, AST 20 U/L), and HbA1c is 5.6%. No prior malignancy. HIV, HBV, and HCV negative. No history of autoimmune disease. No stroke or myocardial infarction in the past 6 months. Normal bladder function, no catheterization required. No prior pelvic radiation. No prior anti-PD-1/PD-L1 or ADC therapy. No recent live vaccine. Pathology report from the TURBT specimen confirms pure urothelial carcinoma with no small cell or neuroendocrine component.

A 68-year-old man was diagnosed with muscle-invasive bladder cancer (predominantly urothelial histology) via TURBT performed 3 weeks ago. Chest CT and CTU showed no evidence of metastasis (N0M0). ECOG performance status is 0, assessed 2 days ago. Recent screening labs (7 days ago) show adequate renal and hepatic function (creatinine 0.9 mg/dL, ALT 22 U/L, AST 20 U/L), and HbA1c is 5.6%. No prior malignancy. HIV, HBV, and HCV negative. No history of autoimmune disease. No stroke or myocardial infarction in the past 6 months. Normal bladder function, no catheterization required. ECOG performance status is 0, assessed 2 days ago. Recent screening labs (7 days ago) show adequate renal and hepatic function (creatinine 0.9 mg/dL, ALT 22 U/L, AST 20 U/L), and HbA1c is 5.6%. No prior malignancy. HIV, HBV, and HCV negative. No history of autoimmune disease. No stroke or myocardial infarction in the past 6 months. Normal bladder function, no catheterization required. No prior pelvic radiation. No prior anti-PD-1/PD-L1 or ADC therapy. No recent live vaccine. Pathology report from the TURBT specimen confirms pure urothelial carcinoma with no small cell or neuroendocrine component. He received a killed influenza vaccine 10 days ago and started prednisone 15 mg/day 6 days ago for a presumed inflammatory reaction at the biopsy site.

No prior anti-PD-1/PD-L1 or ADC therapy. No recent live vaccine. Pathology report from the TURBT specimen confirms pure urothelial carcinoma with no small cell or neuroendocrine component.

A 55-year-old woman has had heart failure with reduced ejection fraction for 8 months, clinically unchanged over the past 3 months with no new symptoms or hospitalizations (chronically stable HFrEF). Renal function testing 5 days ago showed eGFR 68 mL/min/1.73m^2. NYHA class III. A cardiopulmonary exercise risk assessment 1 week ago classified her as low risk. She is 55 years old. Blood pressure is 132/84 on current therapy. A resting ECG 3 days ago showed normal sinus rhythm with no ectopy. Only mild mitral regurgitation on echocardiogram, no significant valvular disease. No history of DVT or PE. No history of syncope. Hemoglobin is 12.1 g/dL. Thyroid function tests are within normal limits. No severe pulmonary disease, no psychiatric illness, and no musculoskeletal disease limiting rehabilitation.

text: A 72-year-old woman was diagnosed with Alzheimer's disease 2 years ago based on positive CSF amyloid biomarkers. She currently has mild cognitive impairment, with a Clinical Dementia Rating (CDR) of 0.5 documented at her last visit 1 month ago. ALT and AST are within normal range. Total bilirubin is 0.8 mg/dL. HIV, HBV, HCV negative. Blood pressure was 128/82 after 10 minutes of rest at screening. eGFR is 72 mL/min/1.73m^2. A recent ECG showed no clinically significant abnormalities. No psychiatric history, no significant risk of suicide. No history of bleeding diathesis or coagulopathy. No brain or spinal disease affecting IT injection or LP procedures. No seizures in the past 6 months.

A 61-year-old woman was diagnosed with rheumatoid arthritis 8 years ago per EULAR/ACR 2010 classification criteria. Her most recent DAS28, calculated 2 weeks ago, is 4.3. She has been on Rituximab maintenance therapy for 3 years, having completed the initial cycle of 2 infusions. Her last Rituximab infusion was 11 months ago. She is currently on prednisolone 5 mg/day, unchanged for the past 6 weeks. She is enrolled in the national social insurance system. She has no rheumatic autoimmune disease other than RA. She was treated for latent tuberculosis 5 years ago and completed the full course. HBV and HCV are negative. Hemoglobin is 13.2 g/dL, neutrophils 2.1 G/L, IgM 0.6 g/L, IgG 7.2 g/L.

A 34-year-old man was clinically diagnosed with retinitis pigmentosa 5 years ago, with a classic presentation of night blindness and progressive peripheral visual field loss confirmed on formal visual field testing. Genetic panel testing performed 3 months ago confirmed biallelic pathogenic variants in the CNGA1 gene, with no other variants of note. Ellipsoid zone length in the study eye, measured on OCT 1 week ago, is 3200 um. No ocular surgery or subretinal implant in the past 6 months. HbA1c is 6.1%. No history of herpes simplex keratitis. No prior AAV gene therapy. No bleeding or coagulation disorders.
